dish_name: Leche de Tigre with Classic Ceviche
description: This authentic Leche de Tigre recipe is the bold, zesty heart of Peru’s iconic ceviche. Made with fresh lime juice, fish stock, aromatics, and optional ají amarillo paste, this vibrant marinade “cooks” raw fish with bright acidity and umami depth. Whether you’re sipping it straight or using it in ceviche, this tangy preparation will awaken your palate and transport you to the Peruvian coast.
ingredients: [{"name": "fresh-squeezed lime juice", "unit": "cups", "amount": "1¾"}, {"name": "fish stock", "unit": "cups", "amount": "1¼"}, {"name": "garlic cloves", "unit": "pieces", "amount": "3"}, {"name": "shallots", "unit": "pieces", "amount": "1 large or 2 small"}, {"name": "kosher salt", "unit": "teaspoons", "amount": "2½"}, {"name": "celery ribs", "unit": "pieces", "amount": "1½"}, {"name": "cilantro stems", "unit": "tablespoon", "amount": "1"}, {"name": "fresh ginger", "unit": "tablespoons", "amount": "2"}, {"name": "ají amarillo paste", "unit": "tablespoons", "amount": "1–2"}, {"name": "fresh white saltwater fish (like sea bass or snapper), cubed", "unit": "lb", "amount": "1"}, {"name": "leche de tigre", "unit": "cups", "amount": "4"}, {"name": "large red onion, thinly sliced", "unit": "piece", "amount": "½"}, {"name": "fresh cilantro leaves, chopped", "unit": "cup", "amount": "¼"}]
instructions: Add lime juice, fish stock, garlic, shallots, salt, celery, cilantro stems, ginger, and ají amarillo paste to a blender. Blend until completely smooth. Strain through a fine-mesh sieve to achieve a silky texture. Cut the fish into uniform, bite-sized cubes. Keep it well chilled until ready to use. In a large mixing bowl, combine the cubed fish, leche de tigre, sliced red onion, and chopped cilantro. Gently mix to coat the fish evenly. Allow it to marinate for 5–10 minutes. Serve the ceviche cold, ideally with sweet potato, boiled corn, or plantain chips for a traditional Peruvian touch.
notes: Use only sushi-grade fish to ensure safety when consuming raw. Optional heat: Add more ají amarillo or use chili for spicier versions. Leftover leche de tigre makes a flavorful shot or base for seafood cocktails. No need to finely chop ingredients — the blender will take care of it. Make ahead tip: Prepare leche de tigre up to a day in advance and refrigerate.
tags: Peruvian, ceviche, leche de tigre, appetizer, main course, seafood, marinade
